title: run_verify: per-suite timeout (BEE_VERIFY_SUITE_TIMEOUT_MS, default 300s) + periodic heartbeat naming still-running suites
action: Per D2: runOne() (:535-561) spawns each suite with full buffering, no timeout, no progress. Add (a) a per-suite timeout — default 300000 ms, env override BEE_VERIFY_SUITE_TIMEOUT_MS, 0/'none' disables — that kills the child process group on expiry and reports the suite as a distinct 'TIMEOUT after <n>ms' failure (never a silent hang, never conflated with an ordinary red); (b) a heartbeat line to stderr every ~30 s while suites run, naming the suites still in flight and elapsed time, so a watching agent never reads a long run as frozen. The hermetic env scrub and concurrency semantics are untouched. Add scripts/test_verify_timeout.mjs: a fixture child script that sleeps past a tiny configured timeout must be killed and reported TIMEOUT; a fast suite under the same config passes; heartbeat emission is asserted (cadence configurable/injectable for the test — do not sleep 30s in tests). PLAN-CHECK W2: NO manual registration — run_verify.mjs auto-discovers scripts/test_*.mjs via DISCOVERY_ROOTS glob (:42-47, :315-334); adding an EXTRA_SUITES entry would DOUBLE-RUN the suite. Just confirm discovery picks it up (node scripts/run_verify.mjs --only test_verify_timeout finds it). If the sleeping fixture child must not be discovered as a suite itself, name it so the test_*.mjs glob does not match (e.g. scripts/fixtures/sleep_child.mjs).

truths: a hung suite ends as a reported TIMEOUT failure, never an infinite silent wait
default behavior for fast suites is unchanged
heartbeat goes to stderr and never corrupts machine-readable stdout output
prohibitions: do not change the hermetic env scrub
do not alter suite pass/fail semantics for non-timeout cases
no unconditional 30s sleeps in the new test

trace.outcome: Added per-suite timeout (BEE_VERIFY_SUITE_TIMEOUT_MS, default 300000ms, 0/none disables) and a stderr heartbeat to run_verify.mjs's runOne(); a hung suite is killed via its WHOLE process group (kills grandchildren too) and reported as a distinct TIMEOUT, never conflated with an ordinary FAIL; hermetic env scrub and non-timeout pass/fail semantics untouched. New scripts/test_verify_timeout.mjs (auto-discovered, no manual registration) covers timeout, group-kill, and injectable heartbeat emission with no unconditional 30s sleeps.
